You are editing lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc in /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster.

Your job: Add AXLA voicing annotations to local operations anchors and purge dead cmk_* vocabulary from inline text in the Local Operations and Script Internals sections.

## Task 1: Add annotations to Local Operations anchors (lines ~700-794)

For each `[[anchor]]` line in the Local Operations section, add a `// ⟦axl_voices ...⟧` annotation line BETWEEN the anchor and the heading. The GCP operations already have this pattern — follow it exactly.

Current pattern (MISSING annotation):
```
[[opss_sentry_start]]
=== {opss_sentry_start}
```

Target pattern (WITH annotation):
```
[[opss_sentry_start]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧
=== {opss_sentry_start}
```

**Annotation assignments:**

Top-level operations (user-invoked from console):
- `[[opss_sentry_start]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`
- `[[opbs_bottle_start]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`
- `[[opbr_bottle_run]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`

Sub-sequences (ordered steps within a parent operation):

- `[[mkr_command_exec]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_sequence axe_bash_interactive⟧`

VM management operations:
- `[[ops_rbv_check]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`
- `[[ops_rbv_mirror]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`

Utility (inline, no subdoc):
- `[[mkc_interface_check]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_scripted⟧`

## Task 2: Add annotations to Script Internals anchors (lines ~2498-2533)

Script phases (run inside sentry container):
- `[[scr_security_config]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_scripted⟧`

- `[[scr_port_setup]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_sequence axe_bash_scripted⟧`
- `[[scr_access_setup]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_sequence axe_bash_scripted⟧`
- `[[scr_dns_step]]` → `// ⟦axl_voices axo_sequence axe_bash_scripted⟧`

## Task 3: Purge cmk_* from RBSA inline text

Search the Local Operations section (~lines 698-794) for any references to `{at_rbm_console}`, `{cmk_recipe_line_s}`, `{cmk_script_line_s}`, `{cmk_external_rule}`, or any `cmk_` prefixed terms. Remove or replace them with neutral phrasing.

For example, if text says "within the {at_rbm_console}", just remove that phrase or replace with something like "within the deployment console" without using a term reference.

Also check the Script Internals section (~lines 2493-2543) for the same.

## IMPORTANT
- Read the file sections BEFORE editing
- Use the Edit tool for each change
- Do NOT touch any lines outside the Local Operations section (698-794) and Script Internals section (2493-2543)
- Do NOT modify any include:: directives
- Do NOT modify the mapping section at the top of the file
- Preserve all existing content — only ADD annotation lines and REMOVE/REPLACE cmk_* references

   376→// ============================================================================
   377→// TIER 1: WHY AND WHAT
   378→// ============================================================================
   379→
   380→== System Overview
   381→
   382→=== Core Trust Challenge
   383→
   384→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   385→
   386→{st_container}-
   387→based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   388→
   389→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   390→
   391→* {st_container_s}
   392→provide powerful tool isolation
   393→* But they need controlled access
   394→to do useful work
   395→* Standard
   396→{st_container}
   397→networking
   398→grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   399→
   400→=== The Bottle Pattern
   401→
   402→{at_rbm_system}
   403→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   404→{st_container}
   405→type here referred to as a
   406→{at_bottle_container}:
   407→
   408→. File Access Boundary
   409→* {at_bottle_container}
   410→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   411→{st_volume_mount_s}
   412→* No other access
   413→to
   414→{at_workstation}
   415→filesystem
   416→
   417→. Network Boundary
   418→* {at_bottle_container}
   419→has no direct network access
   420→* All traffic
   421→flows through dedicated
   422→{at_sentry_container},
   423→a
   424→{st_container}
   425→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   426→* Only declared
   427→{st_port_map_s}
   428→and destinations
   429→allowed
   430→* Full logging
   431→of allowed and denied traffic
   432→
   433→Each
   434→{at_bottle_service}
   435→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   436→
   437→* One
   438→{at_sentry_container}
   439→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   440→* One
   441→{at_censer_container}
   442→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   443→* One
   444→{at_bottle_container}
   445→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   446→* Clear configuration
   447→in a single
   448→{at_nameplate}
   449→file
   450→
   451→The
   452→{at_censer_container}
   453→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   454→{at_sentry_container},
   455→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   456→{at_bottle_container}
   457→via `--net=container:censer`.
   458→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   459→{at_bottle_container}
   460→experiences only a functional path to its
   461→{at_sentry_container}
   462→gateway
   463→
   464→=== Security Properties
   465→
   466→The
   467→{at_rbm_system}
   468→maintains following essential guarantees:
   469→
   470→. File Access Control
   471→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   472→can access only explicitly configured
   473→{st_volume_mount_s}
   474→* No access to broader system resources
   475→
   476→. Network Isolation
   477→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   478→{at_sentry_container}
   479→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   480→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   481→* Only explicitly configured
   482→{st_port_map_s}
   483→allow traffic between
   484→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network}
   485→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   486→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   487→{at_sentry_container_s}
   488→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   489→
   490→. Service Separation
   491→* Each
   492→{at_bottle_service}
   493→fully isolated
   494→* No cross-service communication
   495→* Independent security policies
   496→* Clean state separation
   497→
   498→. Service Access Control
   499→* External access to services only permitted when {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled
   500→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   501→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   502→* Entry traffic flows: external -> pod ->
   503→{at_sentry_container} -> {at_bottle_container}
   504→
   505→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   506→
   507→== Getting Started
   508→
   509→For builders and operators new to {at_recipe_bottle}, this section provides conceptual grounding and the setup journey.
   510→
   511→include::RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc[leveloffset=+2]
   512→
   513→// ============================================================================
   514→// TIER 2: HOW
   515→// ============================================================================
   516→
   517→== Google API Engagement
   518→
   519→{at_recipe_bottle}
   520→invokes
   521→{gcp_service}
   522→APIs through
   523→{giam_service_account}
   524→JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.
   525→
   526→* *Authentication*:
   527→{giam_service_account_s}
   528→use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
   529→These JWTs are exchanged for
   530→{oauth_token_s}
   531→via Google's token endpoint.
   532→All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.
   533→
   534→* *Invocation Pattern*:
   535→Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
   536→Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
   537→Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.
   538→
   539→* *Long-Running Operations*:
   540→{gcp_service}
   541→{gcp_lro_s}
   542→return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
   543→The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
   544→The system handles both success and error states.
   545→Operations utilize
   546→{rbbc_submit}
   547→and
   548→{rbbc_await}
   549→to handle all asynchronous operations.
   550→
   551→* *IAM Policies*:
   552→All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
   553→{gcp_service}
   554→resources.
   555→
   556→== Operational Invariants
   557→
   558→=== Concurrent Session Safety
   559→
   560→{at_recipe_bottle}
   561→commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
   562→This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.
   563→
   564→Concurrency safety is achieved through:
   565→
   566→* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
   567→Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BUD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.
   568→
   569→* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
   570→Cloud resources (source tarballs, build IDs) incorporate the invocation timestamp, preventing overwrite conflicts in
   571→{rbtgi_build_bucket}
   572→and
   573→{rbtgi_repository}.
   574→
   575→* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
   576→Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
   577→The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.
   578→
   579→* *Semantic Preconditions*:
   580→Operations like
   581→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   582→require clean git state.
   583→This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.
   584→
   585→NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
   586→Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.

   379→
   380→== System Overview
   381→
   382→=== Core Trust Challenge
   383→
   384→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   385→
   386→{st_container}-
   387→based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   388→
   389→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   390→
   391→* {st_container_s}
   392→provide powerful tool isolation
   393→* But they need controlled access
   394→to do useful work
   395→* Standard
   396→{st_container}
   397→networking
   398→grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   399→
   400→=== The Bottle Pattern
   401→
   402→{at_rbm_system}
   403→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   404→{st_container}
   405→type here referred to as a
   406→{at_bottle_container}:
   407→
   408→. File Access Boundary
   409→* {at_bottle_container}
   410→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   411→{st_volume_mount_s}
   412→* No other access
   413→to
   414→{at_workstation}
   415→filesystem
   416→
   417→. Network Boundary
   418→* {at_bottle_container}
   419→has no direct network access
   420→* All traffic
   421→flows through dedicated
   422→{at_sentry_container},
   423→a
   424→{st_container}
   425→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   426→* Only declared
   427→{st_port_map_s}
   428→and destinations
   429→allowed
   430→* Full logging
   431→of allowed and denied traffic
   432→
   433→Each
   434→{at_bottle_service}
   435→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   436→
   437→* One
   438→{at_sentry_container}
   439→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   440→* One
   441→{at_censer_container}
   442→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   443→* One
   444→{at_bottle_container}
   445→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   446→* Clear configuration
   447→in a single
   448→{at_nameplate}
   449→file
   450→
   451→The
   452→{at_censer_container}
   453→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   454→{at_sentry_container},
   455→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   456→{at_bottle_container}
   457→via `--net=container:censer`.
   458→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   459→{at_bottle_container}
   460→experiences only a functional path to its
   461→{at_sentry_container}
   462→gateway
   463→
   464→=== Security Properties
   465→
   466→The
   467→{at_rbm_system}
   468→maintains following essential guarantees:
   469→
   470→. File Access Control
   471→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   472→can access only explicitly configured
   473→{st_volume_mount_s}
   474→* No access to broader system resources
   475→
   476→. Network Isolation
   477→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   478→{at_sentry_container}
   479→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   480→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   481→* Only explicitly configured
   482→{st_port_map_s}
   483→allow traffic between
   484→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network}
   485→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   486→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   487→{at_sentry_container_s}
   488→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   489→
   490→. Service Separation
   491→* Each
   492→{at_bottle_service}
   493→fully isolated
   494→* No cross-service communication
   495→* Independent security policies
   496→* Clean state separation
   497→
   498→. Service Access Control
   499→* External access to services only permitted when {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled
   500→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   501→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   502→* Entry traffic flows: external -> pod ->
   503→{at_sentry_container} -> {at_bottle_container}
   504→
   505→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   506→

   508→

   516→
   517→== Google API Engagement
   518→
   519→{at_recipe_bottle}
   520→invokes
   521→{gcp_service}
   522→APIs through
   523→{giam_service_account}
   524→JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.
   525→
   526→* *Authentication*:
   527→{giam_service_account_s}
   528→use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
   529→These JWTs are exchanged for
   530→{oauth_token_s}
   531→via Google's token endpoint.
   532→All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.
   533→
   534→* *Invocation Pattern*:
   535→Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
   536→Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
   537→Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.
   538→
   539→* *Long-Running Operations*:
   540→{gcp_service}
   541→{gcp_lro_s}
   542→return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
   543→The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
   544→The system handles both success and error states.
   545→Operations utilize
   546→{rbbc_submit}
   547→and
   548→{rbbc_await}
   549→to handle all asynchronous operations.
   550→
   551→* *IAM Policies*:
   552→All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
   553→{gcp_service}
   554→resources.
   555→
   556→== Operational Invariants
   557→
   558→=== Concurrent Session Safety
   559→
   560→{at_recipe_bottle}
   561→commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
   562→This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.
   563→
   564→Concurrency safety is achieved through:
   565→
   566→* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
   567→Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BUD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.
   568→
   569→* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
   570→Cloud resources (source tarballs, build IDs) incorporate the invocation timestamp, preventing overwrite conflicts in
   571→{rbtgi_build_bucket}
   572→and
   573→{rbtgi_repository}.
   574→
   575→* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
   576→Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
   577→The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.
   578→
   579→* *Semantic Preconditions*:
   580→Operations like
   581→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   582→require clean git state.
   583→This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.
   584→
   585→NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
   586→Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.

   781→[[mkc_interface_check]]
   782→==== {mkc_interface_check}
   783→
   784→A utility that verifies network interface presence and configuration within a container context:
   785→
   786→[source,makefile]
   787→----
   788→# Waits until interface has an IPv4 address assigned, fails after timeout
   789→# @param 1 interface   Network interface name to check
   790→# @param 2 timeout_s   Number of seconds to wait before failing
   791→define check_interface
   792→timeout $(2)s sh -c "while ! ip addr show $(1) | grep -q \"inet \"; do sleep 0.2; done"
   793→endef
   794→----
   795→
   796→This utility generates precise validation commands for network interface readiness probes, accepting an interface name and timeout duration as parameters.

   802→== Term Definitions
   803→
   804→=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms
   805→
   806→[[at_recipe_bottle]]
   807→{at_recipe_bottle}::
   808→The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
   809→{gcp_service}
   810→infrastructure.
   811→
   812→=== RB Role Infrastructure
   813→
   814→[[rbtr_role]]
   815→// ⟦axl_voices axo_identity⟧
   816→{rbtr_role}::
   817→An operational identity within the
   818→{at_recipe_bottle}
   819→system
   820→that an
   821→{at_operator}
   822→can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
   823→Each
   824→{rbtr_role}
   825→is implemented through a dedicated
   826→{giam_service_account}
   827→and corresponding
   828→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   829→containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
   830→{rbtr_mason}
   831→which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.
   832→
   833→=== {giam_service_account} Definitions
   834→
   835→[[rbtr_payor]]
   836→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   837→{rbtr_payor}::
   838→An
   839→{rbtr_role}
   840→that enables an
   841→{at_operator}
   842→to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
   843→The
   844→{rbtr_payor}
   845→operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.
   846→
   847→[[rbtr_governor]]
   848→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   849→{rbtr_governor}::
   850→An
   851→{rbtr_role}
   852→that grants an
   853→{at_operator}
   854→{gcp_project_id}
   855→-scope Owner permissions without billing authority, designed for automation-friendly operations.
   856→
   857→[[rbtr_retriever]]
   858→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   859→{rbtr_retriever}::
   860→An
   861→{rbtr_role}
   862→that allows an
   863→{at_operator}
   864→read-only access to the
   865→{rbtgi_repository}
   866→for pulling
   867→{rbtgi_image_s}.
   868→
   869→[[rbtr_mason]]
   870→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   871→{rbtr_mason}::
   872→A cloud-native
   873→{giam_service_account}
   874→that operates exclusively within the
   875→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   876→to execute
   877→{at_recipe_bottle}
   878→build instructions from the
   879→{rbtr_director}.
   880→
   881→The
   882→{rbtr_mason}
   883→impersonates the implicit service agent for builds, replacing Google's auto-created service agent with deterministic permissions and predictable authorization.
   884→
   885→The
   886→{rbtr_mason}
   887→reads from the
   888→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   889→carries out the build process within
   890→{gcb_service},
   891→and publishes
   892→{rbtgi_image_s}
   893→and
   894→{rbtgi_metadata_s}
   895→to the
   896→{rbtgi_repository}
   897→without requiring a local
   898→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   899→since it operates entirely within the cloud infrastructure.
   900→
   901→[[rbtr_director]]
   902→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   903→{rbtr_director}::
   904→An
   905→{rbtr_role}
   906→that grants an
   907→{at_operator}
   908→full build lifecycle permissions including Cloud Build submission and
   909→{rbtgi_repository}
   910→management.
   911→
   912→=== {gcp_service} Definitions
   913→
   914→[[rbtgi_depot_project]]
   915→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
   916→{rbtgi_depot_project}::
   917→The dedicated
   918→{gcp_service}
   919→project instance containing all Recipe Bottle depot resources, APIs, and
   920→{giam_service_account_s}
   921→for container building and management. Created and managed by the
   922→{rbtr_payor}
   923→role.
   924→
   925→[[rbtgi_depot]]
   926→{rbtgi_depot}::
   927→A cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure comprising a
   928→{rbtgi_depot_project},
   929→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   930→{rbtgi_repository},
   931→and
   932→{rbtr_mason}
   933→service account that share their productive lifecycle. Created as a unit by
   934→{rbtgo_depot_create}
   935→and destroyed as a unit by
   936→{rbtgo_depot_destroy}.
   937→
   938→[[rbtgi_payor_project]]
   939→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
   940→{rbtgi_payor_project}::
   941→The administrative
   942→{gcp_service}
   943→project that hosts the
   944→{rbtr_payor}
   945→{giam_service_account}.
   946→This
   947→{gcp_project_id}
   948→serves as the control plane for creating and managing multiple
   949→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   950→instances.
   951→
   952→
   953→[[rbtgi_build_bucket]]
   954→// ⟦axl_voices axig_bucket⟧
   955→{rbtgi_build_bucket}::
   956→The dedicated
   957→{gcs_bucket}
   958→within the
   959→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   960→that holds
   961→{at_recipe_bottle}
   962→build artifacts with configured lifecycle policies.
   963→
   964→=== {gar_service} Definitions
   965→
   966→[[rbtgi_repository]]
   967→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_repository⟧
   968→{rbtgi_repository}::
   969→The managed
   970→{gar_service}
   971→repository within the
   972→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   973→that stores
   974→{at_recipe_bottle}
   975→{rbtgi_image_s}
   976→and metadata.
   977→
   978→[[rbtgi_image]]
   979→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
   980→{rbtgi_image}::
   981→A
   982→{at_recipe_bottle}
   983→container image stored in the
   984→{rbtgi_repository}

   986→
   987→[[rbtgi_metadata]]
   988→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
   989→{rbtgi_metadata}::
   990→A compressed archive containing
   991→{at_recipe_bottle}
   992→build transcript, SBOM, and provenance data stored in the
   993→{rbtgi_repository}
   994→as a Generic Artifact.
   995→
   996→=== Ark Definitions
   997→
   998→[[rbtga_ark]]
   999→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_group⟧
  1000→{rbtga_ark}::
  1001→The coherent unit of a
  1002→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1003→build output: a paired set of artifacts in the
  1004→{rbtgi_repository}
  1005→sharing a common
  1006→{rbtga_consecration}
  1007→but distinguished by suffix.
  1008→An ark comprises the
  1009→{rbtga_ark_image}
  1010→(`-image` suffix) containing the container image and the
  1011→{rbtga_ark_about}
  1012→(`-about` suffix) containing build provenance and metadata.
  1013→Arks are created atomically by
  1014→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
  1015→and abjured atomically by
  1016→{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.
  1017→
  1018→[[rbtga_consecration]]
  1019→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_timestamp⟧
  1020→{rbtga_consecration}::
  1021→The timestamp-based identifier marking when a vessel was consecrated into an
  1022→{rbtga_ark}.
  1023→Format: `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` or similar timestamp pattern.
  1024→Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags: `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-image` and `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-about`.
  1025→
  1026→[[rbtga_ark_image]]
  1027→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
  1028→{rbtga_ark_image}::
  1029→The container image artifact within an
  1030→{rbtga_ark},
  1031→tagged with the `-image` suffix.
  1032→Contains the executable container layers built by
  1033→{gcb_service}.
  1034→
  1035→[[rbtga_ark_about]]
  1036→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
  1037→{rbtga_ark_about}::
  1038→The metadata artifact within an
  1039→{rbtga_ark},
  1040→tagged with the `-about` suffix.
  1041→Contains build provenance, SBOM (Software Bill of Materials), and build transcript assembled during
  1042→{rbtgo_trigger_build}.
  1043→
  1044→=== Support Infrastructure Definitions
  1045→
  1046→[[giam_binding]]
  1047→// ⟦axl_voices axig_binding⟧
  1048→{giam_binding}::
  1049→An association between a
  1050→{giam_service_account}
  1051→and a specific permission role within a resource scope.
  1052→
  1053→[[oauth_token]]
  1054→{oauth_token}::
  1055→A time-limited credential exchanged from
  1056→{giam_service_account}

  1058→
  1059→
  1060→[[rbtgi_rbra_file]]
  1061→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
  1062→{rbtgi_rbra_file}::
  1063→A bash-sourceable environment file containing Google
  1064→{giam_service_account}

  1071→Loaded via
  1072→{rbtoe_rbra_load}

  1074→
  1075→[[rbtgi_json_key_file]]
  1076→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
  1077→{rbtgi_json_key_file}::
  1078→A Google
  1079→{giam_service_account}
  1080→JSON key file in its native format as downloaded from the Google Cloud Console, containing the complete credential structure including private_key, client_email, project_id, and other metadata required for authentication.
  1081→
  1082→=== {gcp_service} Core Definitions

  1183→
  1184→=== Cross-Reference Definitions
  1185→
  1186→[[at_rbm_system]]
  1187→{at_rbm_system}::

  1189→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1190→system enabling secure container management.

  1193→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧

  1196→
  1197→[[rbtr_consumer]]
  1198→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
  1199→{rbtr_consumer}::
  1200→An
  1201→{rbtr_role}

  1203→{at_operator}

  1205→{at_bottle_image_s}
  1206→on their
  1207→{at_workstation}.
  1208→The
  1209→{rbtr_consumer}
  1210→interacts with bottle and sentry containers to accomplish work, distinct from roles that build and deploy the system.
  1211→
  1212→[[at_operator]]
  1213→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
  1214→{at_operator}::

  1216→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1217→system on their
  1218→{at_workstation}

  1220→{rbtgi_rbra_file}

  1222→{rbtr_role_s}.

  1227→{at_operator_s}

  1229→{at_rbm_system}
  1230→commands.
  1231→
  1232→[[at_base_utilities]]
  1233→// ⟦axl_voices axo_dependency⟧
  1234→{at_base_utilities}::

  1236→(`bash`, `git`, `curl`, `openssh`, `jq`)
  1237→required on the
  1238→{at_workstation}.
  1239→
  1240→=== Local Architecture Definitions
  1241→
  1242→[[at_sessile_service]]
  1243→{at_sessile_service}::
  1244→A long-running
  1245→{at_bottle_service}
  1246→that starts via
  1247→{opbs_bottle_start}
  1248→and persists until explicitly stopped.
  1249→The sessile pattern is used for services that maintain state and provide ongoing functionality.
  1250→
  1251→[[at_agile_service]]
  1252→{at_agile_service}::
  1253→A transient
  1254→{at_bottle_service}
  1255→that executes via
  1256→{opbr_bottle_run}
  1257→and terminates when its command completes.
  1258→The agile pattern is used for one-shot operations that produce output and exit.
  1259→
  1260→[[at_bottle_service]]
  1261→{at_bottle_service}::
  1262→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1263→{at_sentry_container},
  1264→one
  1265→{at_censer_container},
  1266→and one
  1267→{at_bottle_container}
  1268→working together to provide secure functionality.
  1269→Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1270→
  1271→[[at_sentry_container]]
  1272→{at_sentry_container}::
  1273→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1274→{at_bottle_service}.
  1275→It connects to both
  1276→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network},
  1277→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1278→
  1279→[[at_censer_container]]
  1280→{at_censer_container}::
  1281→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1282→{at_bottle_container}
  1283→starts.
  1284→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1285→{at_sentry_container},
  1286→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1287→{at_bottle_container}
  1288→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1289→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1290→
  1291→[[at_bottle_container]]
  1292→{at_bottle_container}::
  1293→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1294→{at_censer_container}.
  1295→It is connected only to the
  1296→{at_enclave_network}
  1297→and configured with specific
  1298→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1299→All external communication flows through its associated
  1300→{at_sentry_container}.
  1301→
  1302→[[at_enclave_network]]
  1303→{at_enclave_network}::
  1304→An isolated network connecting a
  1305→{at_bottle_container}
  1306→to its
  1307→{at_sentry_container},
  1308→using
  1309→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1310→for addressing.
  1311→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1312→{at_bottle_container}.
  1313→
  1314→[[at_transit_network]]
  1315→{at_transit_network}::
  1316→The network interface that connects a
  1317→{at_sentry_container}
  1318→to the
  1319→{at_workstation_p}
  1320→network resources.
  1321→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1322→
  1323→[[at_nameplate]]
  1324→{at_nameplate}::
  1325→A configuration file that defines all aspects of a
  1326→{at_bottle_service},
  1327→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1328→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1329→{at_rbm_system}
  1330→can instantiate.
  1331→
  1332→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1333→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1334→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1335→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1336→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1337→This program ensures all
  1338→{at_bottle_container}
  1339→network traffic routes through its associated
  1340→{at_sentry_container}
  1341→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1342→
  1343→[[st_container]]
  1344→{st_container}::
  1345→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
  1346→
  1347→[[st_volume_mount]]
  1348→{st_volume_mount}::
  1349→A configuration that allows
  1350→{st_container_s}
  1351→to access specified portions of the host filesystem.
  1352→
  1353→[[st_port_map]]
  1354→{st_port_map}::
  1355→A specification of how network ports should be exposed and mapped between
  1356→{st_container_s}
  1357→and the host system.
  1358→
  1359→[[at_sentry_image]]
  1360→{at_sentry_image}::
  1361→A
  1362→{st_image}
  1363→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1364→{at_sentry_container}.
  1365→
  1366→[[at_moniker]]
  1367→{at_moniker}::
  1368→A unique identifier that distinguishes each
  1369→{at_bottle_service}
  1370→instance within the
  1371→{at_rbm_system}.
  1372→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1373→
  1374→[[at_rbm_console]]
  1375→{at_rbm_console}::
  1376→The primary operational automation that implements the
  1377→{at_rbm_system}
  1378→lifecycle rules for managing
  1379→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1380→
  1381→[[at_rbm_config]]
  1382→{at_rbm_config}::
  1383→Configuration settings for the
  1384→{at_rbm_system},
  1385→including
  1386→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1387→and environment-specific parameters.
  1388→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1389→
  1390→[[at_rbm_secret]]
  1391→{at_rbm_secret}::
  1392→A sensitive configuration value needed by the
  1393→{at_rbm_system},
  1394→such as registry credentials.
  1395→Kept separate from version control.
  1396→
  1397→[[at_rbm_repo]]
  1398→{at_rbm_repo}::
  1399→The version control repository containing the
  1400→{at_rbm_system}
  1401→implementation, including operational automation,
  1402→{at_nameplate_s},
  1403→and documentation.
  1404→
  1405→[[at_user_repo]]
  1406→{at_user_repo}::
  1407→A version control repository owned by an
  1408→{at_operator}
  1409→where the
  1410→{at_rbm_system}
  1411→is instantiated.
  1412→The
  1413→{at_operator}
  1414→configures it by setting up regime files and creating
  1415→{at_nameplate_s}
  1416→to define
  1417→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1418→
  1419→[[at_container_registry]]
  1420→{at_container_registry}::
  1421→A service that stores and provides access to
  1422→{st_published_image_s}.
  1423→
  1424→[[at_startup_script]]
  1425→{at_startup_script}::
  1426→A shell script that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1427→{at_bottle_service}.
  1428→
  1429→[[at_stash_machine]]
  1430→{at_stash_machine}::
  1431→A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
  1432→Removed after completing its specific tasks.
  1433→
  1434→[[at_operational_machine]]
  1435→{at_operational_machine}::
  1436→The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all
  1437→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1438→and their associated containers.
  1439→
  1440→[[at_transit_network_name]]
  1441→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1442→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1443→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1444→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-uplink`.
  1445→
  1446→[[at_enclave_network_name]]
  1447→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1448→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1449→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1450→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-enclave`.
  1451→
  1452→[[at_enclave_namespace_name]]
  1453→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1454→The unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1455→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1456→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-namespace`.
  1457→
  1458→[[at_bottle_container_name]]
  1459→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1460→The unique container name formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1461→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1462→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle`.
  1463→
  1464→[[at_sentry_container_name]]
  1465→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1466→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1467→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1468→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry`.
  1469→
  1470→[[st_dockerfile]]
  1471→{st_dockerfile}::
  1472→A specification file that defines how to build a
  1473→{st_image}.
  1474→
  1475→[[st_published_image]]
  1476→{st_published_image}::
  1477→A
  1478→{st_image}
  1479→that has been uploaded to a
  1480→{at_container_registry}.
  1481→
  1482→[[st_image]]
  1483→{st_image}::
  1484→A template used to create
  1485→{st_container}
  1486→instances, containing the service's code, runtime, and dependencies.
  1487→
  1488→[[st_image_store]]
  1489→{st_image_store}::
  1490→The local storage location for
  1491→{st_image_s}
  1492→on the
  1493→{at_operator_p}
  1494→{at_workstation}.
  1495→
  1496→[[st_subnet]]
  1497→{st_subnet}::
  1498→A logically visible subdivision of IP network address space.
  1499→
  1500→[[st_gateway]]
  1501→{st_gateway}::
  1502→A network node that serves as the entry point between two networks.

  1541→=== Repository Regime (RBRR)
  1542→
  1543→The
  1544→regime
  1545→defines installation-wide configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all
  1546→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1547→operations.
  1548→This specification documents only the subset of RBRR variables relevant to
  1549→{gcp_service}
  1550→operations.
  1551→
  1552→[[rbrr_regime]]
  1553→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  1554→{rbrr_regime}::
  1555→An
  1556→regime
  1557→that defines repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings shared across all
  1558→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1559→operations.
  1560→RBRR regimes contain depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths that establish the foundational infrastructure configuration.
  1561→
  1562→[[rbrr_depot_project_id]]
  1563→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id⟧
  1564→{rbrr_depot_project_id}::
  1565→The
  1566→{gcp_service}
  1567→{gcp_project_id}
  1568→where all
  1569→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1570→resources are created and managed.
  1571→Used as the target
  1572→{gcp_project_id}
  1573→in API calls and as the audience claim in
  1574→{oauth_token}
  1575→exchange.
  1576→
  1577→[[rbrr_gcp_region]]
  1578→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_region⟧
  1579→{rbrr_gcp_region}::
  1580→The default
  1581→{gcp_service}
  1582→region for regional services like
  1583→{gar_service}
  1584→and
  1585→{gcb_service}.
  1586→Applied when creating repositories and submitting builds unless overridden by operation-specific configuration.
  1587→
  1588→[[rbrr_gar_repository]]
  1589→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id⟧
  1590→{rbrr_gar_repository}::
  1591→The
  1592→{gar_service}
  1593→{rbtgi_repository}
  1594→name within
  1595→{rbrr_depot_project_id}
  1596→that stores all
  1597→{at_bottle_image_s}
  1598→and
  1599→{rbtgi_metadata_s}.
  1600→
  1601→[[rbrr_retriever_rbra_file]]
  1602→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
  1603→{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}::
  1604→Path to an
  1605→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1606→stored in the
  1607→{at_operator_p}
  1608→local filesystem outside the source
  1609→{rbtgi_repository}.
  1610→This file enables the
  1611→{at_operator}
  1612→to assume the
  1613→{rbtr_retriever}
  1614→role for container image operations.
  1615→
  1616→[[rbrr_director_rbra_file]]
  1617→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
  1618→{rbrr_director_rbra_file}::
  1619→Path to an
  1620→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1621→stored in the
  1622→{at_operator_p}
  1623→local filesystem outside the source
  1624→{rbtgi_repository}.
  1625→This file enables the
  1626→{at_operator}
  1627→to assume the
  1628→{rbtr_director}
  1629→for build submission and image management operations.
  1630→
  1631→[[rbrr_governor_rbra_file]]
  1632→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
  1633→{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}::
  1634→Path to an
  1635→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1636→stored in the
  1637→{at_operator_p}
  1638→local filesystem outside the source
  1639→{rbtgi_repository}.
  1640→This file enables the
  1641→{at_operator}
  1642→to assume the
  1643→{rbtr_governor}
  1644→role for
  1645→{gcp_project_id}
  1646→administration operations.
  1647→
  1648→[[rbrr_payor_rbra_file]]
  1649→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
  1650→{rbrr_payor_rbra_file}::
  1651→Path to an
  1652→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1653→stored in the
  1654→{at_operator_p}
  1655→local filesystem outside the source
  1656→{rbtgi_repository}.
  1657→This file enables the
  1658→{at_operator}
  1659→to assume the
  1660→{rbtr_payor}
  1661→role for billing and
  1662→{gcp_project_id}
  1663→lifecycle operations.

  1665→=== Authentication Regime (RBRA)
  1666→
  1667→[[rbra_regime]]
  1668→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  1669→{rbra_regime}::
  1670→An
  1671→regime
  1672→that contains
  1673→{giam_service_account}
  1674→credentials and authentication material for secure access to
  1675→{gcp_service}
  1676→APIs.
  1677→RBRA regimes are security-isolated configuration files typically stored outside the main repository in `../station-files/secrets/rbra-*.env` locations.
  1678→These regimes enable different service account roles to authenticate and operate within their designated scopes.
  1679→
  1680→An {rbtgi_rbra_file} is a credentials file containing the minimum identity and signing material needed for
  1681→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1682→tools to authenticate to
  1683→{gcp_service}
  1684→via
  1685→{giam_service_account}.
  1686→
  1687→[[rbra_client_email]]
  1688→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_service_account⟧
  1689→{rbra_client_email}::
  1690→The
  1691→{giam_service_account}
  1692→email address assigned by
  1693→{gcp_service},
  1694→used as the issuer (`iss`) when constructing JWTs for OAuth2 token exchange.
  1695→
  1696→[[rbra_private_key]]
  1697→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
  1698→{rbra_private_key}::
  1699→The PEM-encoded RSA private key material corresponding to the
  1700→{giam_service_account}.
  1701→Stored as a literal multi-line string with `\n` sequences that are expanded when signing JWTs.
  1702→Used only in memory at runtime; never written separately to disk.
  1703→
  1704→[[rbra_project_id]]
  1705→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id⟧
  1706→{rbra_project_id}::
  1707→The
  1708→{gcp_service}
  1709→{gcp_project_id}
  1710→that owns the
  1711→{giam_service_account}.
  1712→Provides scope for all API operations since a
  1713→{giam_service_account}
  1714→can only act within its parent project's boundaries unless granted cross-project roles.
  1715→
  1716→NOTE: Individual RBRA variables beyond the core documented set are actively used throughout the system but formal definition within this specification is TBD for future documentation efforts.
  1717→
  1718→=== Payor Regime (RBRP)
  1719→
  1720→The
  1721→{rbtr_payor}
  1722→regime
  1723→defines configuration for the administrative layer that manages
  1724→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1725→infrastructure.
  1726→These settings control how the
  1727→{rbtr_payor}
  1728→creates and manages
  1729→{rbtgi_depot_project}
  1730→instances.
  1731→
  1732→[[rbrp_regime]]
  1733→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  1734→{rbrp_regime}::
  1735→An
  1736→regime
  1737→that contains configuration for the administrative
  1738→{rbtr_payor}
  1739→layer managing
  1740→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1741→infrastructure.
  1742→RBRP regimes define payor project identification, billing account linkage, OAuth client configuration, and depot project tracking arrays that enable infrastructure lifecycle management.
  1743→
  1744→[[rbrp_payor_project_id]]
  1745→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id⟧
  1746→{rbrp_payor_project_id}::
  1747→The
  1748→{gcp_service}
  1749→{gcp_project_id}
  1750→that hosts the OAuth 2.0 client configuration for
  1751→{rbtr_payor}
  1752→operations.
  1753→This
  1754→{gcp_project_id}
  1755→must exist before Recipe Bottle initialization and serves as the administrative control plane.
  1756→
  1757→[[rbrp_billing_account_id]]
  1758→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_billing_account⟧
  1759→{rbrp_billing_account_id}::
  1760→The
  1761→{gcp_service}
  1762→billing account identifier that the
  1763→{rbtr_payor}
  1764→links to newly created
  1765→{rbtgi_depot_project}
  1766→instances.
  1767→Format: XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX.
  1768→
  1769→[[rbrp_oauth_client_id]]
  1770→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
  1771→{rbrp_oauth_client_id}::
  1772→The public OAuth 2.0 client identifier for the
  1773→{rbtr_payor}
  1774→desktop application.
  1775→
  1776→[[rbrp_depot_project_ids]]
  1777→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id axd_repeated⟧
  1778→{rbrp_depot_project_ids}::
  1779→Space-delimited array of active depot project IDs managed by this
  1780→{rbtr_payor}.
  1781→
  1782→=== OAuth Regime (RBRO)
  1783→
  1784→The OAuth
  1785→regime
  1786→stores sensitive OAuth credentials locally for
  1787→{rbtr_payor}
  1788→authentication.
  1789→
  1790→[[rbro_regime]]
  1791→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  1792→{rbro_regime}::
  1793→An
  1794→regime
  1795→containing OAuth client credentials and refresh tokens for
  1796→{rbtr_payor}
  1797→operations.
  1798→Stored in `~/.rbw/rbro.env` with 600 permissions, never committed to version control.
  1799→
  1800→[[rbro_client_secret]]
  1801→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
  1802→{rbro_client_secret}::
  1803→The OAuth 2.0 client secret from the Google Cloud Console OAuth client configuration.
  1804→
  1805→[[rbro_refresh_token]]
  1806→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
  1807→{rbro_refresh_token}::
  1808→The long-lived OAuth refresh token obtained during the authorization flow, used to obtain fresh access tokens.
  1809→

  2049→=== Environment Variables Regime (RBEV)
  2050→
  2051→[[rbev_regime]]
  2052→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  2053→{rbev_regime}::
  2054→An
  2055→regime
  2056→that defines runtime container environment variables and configuration parameters for active
  2057→{at_bottle_image}
  2058→instances.
  2059→RBEV regimes specify container execution parameters, port mappings, working directories, and other runtime characteristics that are applied when containers are instantiated.
  2060→These configurations are typically embedded within recipe files and container orchestration specifications.
  2061→
  2062→NOTE: Individual RBEV variables are actively used throughout the system but formal definition within this specification is TBD for future documentation efforts.

  2064→== Type Voicings
  2065→
  2066→Recipe Bottle type voicings define constrained data formats used throughout the system.
  2067→
  2068→[[rbst_xname]]

  2073→

  2075→// ⟦axl_voices axtu_string⟧

  2164→=== Bash Console Control Voicings
  2165→
  2166→Control terms for Recipe Bottle operations executing in a bash shell with human operator present.
  2167→Each term voices the corresponding
  2168→{xref_AXLA}
  2169→control motif in the
  2170→{axe_bash_interactive}
  2171→environment.
  2172→
  2173→[[rbbc_variable]]
  2174→// ⟦axl_voices axc_variable axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2175→{rbbc_variable}::
  2176→A shell variable denoted by guillemets `«NAME»` storing intermediate results.
  2177→Implemented as bash variables or associative array entries.
  2178→
  2179→[[rbbc_fatal]]
  2180→// ⟦axl_voices axc_fatal axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2181→{rbbc_fatal}::
  2182→Red console text followed by `exit 1`.
  2183→Script terminates immediately. Human operator must intervene.
  2184→
  2185→[[rbbc_warn]]
  2186→// ⟦axl_voices axc_warn axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2187→{rbbc_warn}::
  2188→Yellow console text indicating non-blocking concern.
  2189→Script continues execution. Human operator may note for later.
  2190→
  2191→[[rbbc_store]]
  2192→// ⟦axl_voices axc_store axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2193→{rbbc_store}::
  2194→Capture of a value from command output or API response into a
  2195→{rbbc_variable}.
  2196→Typically via bash assignment or jq extraction.
  2197→
  2198→[[rbbc_use]]
  2199→// ⟦axl_voices axc_use axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2200→{rbbc_use}::
  2201→Reference to a previously stored
  2202→{rbbc_variable}
  2203→value.
  2204→Expanded via bash variable substitution.
  2205→
  2206→[[rbbc_require]]
  2207→// ⟦axl_voices axc_require axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2208→{rbbc_require}::
  2209→Validation check that confirms an expected condition.
  2210→Triggers
  2211→{rbbc_fatal}
  2212→if condition not met.
  2213→
  2214→[[rbbc_poll]]
  2215→// ⟦axl_voices axc_poll axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2216→{rbbc_poll}::
  2217→Repeated execution of a command at defined intervals until condition satisfied or timeout.
  2218→Implemented as bash while loop with sleep.
  2219→
  2220→[[rbbc_call]]
  2221→// ⟦axl_voices axc_call axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2222→{rbbc_call}::
  2223→Synchronous invocation of a REST API endpoint via curl.
  2224→Captures HTTP status code and response body. Returns when response received.
  2225→
  2226→[[rbbc_submit]]
  2227→// ⟦axl_voices axc_submit axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2228→{rbbc_submit}::
  2229→Asynchronous invocation of a REST API that returns an LRO operation handle.
  2230→Returns immediately with operation ID for later
  2231→{rbbc_await}.
  2232→
  2233→[[rbbc_await]]
  2234→// ⟦axl_voices axc_await axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2235→{rbbc_await}::
  2236→Blocking wait on a submitted LRO operation until completion.
  2237→Polls operation endpoint until done=true, then returns result.
  2238→
  2239→[[rbbc_show]]
  2240→// ⟦axl_voices axc_show axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2241→{rbbc_show}::
  2242→Presentation of operational facts to the console.
  2243→Formatted output via echo or printf to stdout.
  2244→
  2245→[[rbbc_prompt]]
  2246→// ⟦axl_voices axc_prompt axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2247→{rbbc_prompt}::
  2248→Interactive request for human operator confirmation before proceeding.
  2249→Displays message and waits for user input (typically y/N).
  2250→Script pauses until confirmation received; continues on affirmative, triggers
  2251→{rbbc_fatal}
  2252→on decline or timeout.

  2293→== Orchestration Patterns
  2294→
  2295→Recipe Bottle orchestration patterns define reusable implementation sequences for system operations.
  2296→
  2297→[[rbtoe_rbra_generate]]
  2298→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2299→{rbtoe_rbra_generate}::
  2300→A pattern that converts Google
  2301→{giam_service_account}
  2302→credentials into
  2303→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  2304→format:
  2305→extracts `client_email`, `private_key`, and `project_id` from base64-encoded key data or JSON structure,
  2306→converts to RBRA environment variables (`RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY`, `RBRA_PROJECT_ID`),
  2307→and writes to specified file path with 600 permissions (owner read/write only).
  2308→
  2309→[[rbtoe_rbra_load]]
  2310→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2311→{rbtoe_rbra_load}::
  2312→A pattern that sources an
  2313→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  2314→and validates required fields:
  2315→sources the specified RBRA file into the current environment,
  2316→validates that `RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY`, and `RBRA_PROJECT_ID` are all present and non-empty,
  2317→and triggers
  2318→{rbbc_fatal}
  2319→with standardized error message if any field is missing or invalid.
  2320→
  2321→[[rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange]]
  2322→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2323→{rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange}::
  2324→A pattern that exchanges
  2325→{giam_service_account}
  2326→RBRA credentials for an
  2327→{oauth_token}:
  2328→constructs JWT with RS256 signature using RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY,
  2329→sets JWT claims (iss=RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, aud=https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token, scope=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform),
  2330→POSTs to OAuth2 token endpoint,
  2331→returns access_token from response,
  2332→triggers
  2333→{rbbc_fatal}
  2334→on non-200 HTTP response.
  2335→
  2336→[[rbtoe_api_enable]]
  2337→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2338→{rbtoe_api_enable}::
  2339→A pattern that ensures a specified Google Cloud API is enabled in a project with idempotent behavior:
  2340→
  2341→. attempts to enable the API via serviceusage.services.enable,
  2342→. tolerates HTTP 200 (success) or HTTP 400 with "already enabled" error,
  2343→. executes
  2344→{rbbc_await}
  2345→if Long Running Operation returned,
  2346→. verifies API state is ENABLED via serviceusage.services.get,
  2347→. triggers
  2348→{rbbc_fatal}
  2349→on unexpected errors (409 conflicts, permission denied, etc.),
  2350→. returns success when API is confirmed enabled.
  2351→
  2352→
  2353→[[rbtoe_oauth_refresh]]
  2354→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2355→{rbtoe_oauth_refresh}::
  2356→A pattern that exchanges OAuth refresh token for access token:
  2357→
  2358→. loads RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN and RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET from
  2359→{rbro_regime},
  2360→. loads RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from
  2361→{rbrp_payor_project_id}
  2362→configuration,
  2363→. constructs JSON request with refresh_token, client_id, client_secret, and grant_type=refresh_token,
  2364→. POSTs to https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token with Content-Type: application/json,
  2365→. validates HTTP 200 response or triggers
  2366→{rbbc_fatal}
  2367→with error description,
  2368→. extracts access_token from JSON response,
  2369→. {rbbc_require} access_token is present and non-empty,
  2370→. returns fresh access_token for immediate use,
  2371→. handles 400/401 errors by triggering
  2372→{rbbc_fatal}
  2373→with "OAuth credentials expired or invalid - run
  2374→{rbtgo_payor_refresh}
  2375→".
  2376→
  2377→[[rbtoe_payor_authenticate]]
  2378→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  2379→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}::
  2380→A pattern that establishes
  2381→{rbtr_payor}
  2382→OAuth context:
  2383→
  2384→. executes
  2385→{rbtoe_rbro_load}
  2386→to load OAuth credentials,
  2387→. loads RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from rbrp.env configuration,
  2388→. executes
  2389→{rbtoe_oauth_refresh}
  2390→to obtain access token,
